feedback.text: Good day. I am writing as a non-eu citizen however I am very invested in digital fairness and consumer protection. I would like to see protection against unfair practices done by publishers and the gaming industry. I would like to see protection against publishers killing games and permanently destroying them from being played after end of support. Many beloved titles currently offered playable have a risk of being killed in any time in the future and without a way for those who have purchased it to keep playing. An initiative called Stop Destroying Videogames has been working to combat this massive issue. A community of people have indexed a list of games that are at risk of being killed, or that have already been killed. You can read about them in stopkillinggames.wiki.gg. The initiative has amassed over 1.4 million signatures showcasing how important this issue is to consumers and gamers. We love playing these games and we love them so much that sometimes, amazing people can resurrect dead games by removing drms, opening private servers and modding the game so that it doesn't require third-party license checks so the game can be played. But most of the time, games are beyond rescuing. We are fighting against titans of the industry that not only do they not keep their games that they sold in a playable state, but they actively fight against people who volunteer their time to make good for many and keep the games they love in a playable state. All we want as gamers and consumers is to be able to own games we bought. Thank you for reading.
